name: Real Dialogue Turns Expose Remaining Simulated-Student Fidelity Gaps
statement: Scarlatos et al. benchmark simulated-student dialogue turns against real math tutoring dialogue turns and find that post-trained methods improve several fidelity metrics while important gaps remain.
quantitative_note: Table 1 reports DPO (Llama 3.1 8B) at Acts 0.6840, Knowledge 0.8787, Cos. Sim. 0.7390, ROUGE-L 0.3212, Tutor Resp. 0.2039 versus Zero-Shot (GPT-4.1) at Acts 0.4998, Knowledge 0.8078, Cos. Sim. 0.5460, ROUGE-L 0.1648, Tutor Resp. 0.1911.
caveats: The result compares method packages, because prompting and post-training conditions differ by both workflow and model family.
Error prediction remains weak, and prompt correctness can be misleading when it reflects majority-class correct-response bias.
evaluation_method: Turn-level simulated-student metrics for acts, correctness, errors, knowledge acquisition, text similarity, ROUGE-L, and induced tutor responses.
reported_metrics: Table 1 method-by-metric results for fine-tuning and prompting methods.
limitations: The evidence is specific to tutoring-dialogue turns.
Prompting-vs-post-training effects should not be generalized to all item-response or essay-generation settings.

source_locator.section: arXiv PDF Table 1 and Results Section 4.1
source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases Table 1 and the results discussion comparing simulated turns with real dialogue turns.
